title: chaenorhinum litorale subsp. litorale
intro: Chaenorhinum litorale subsp. litorale, also known as sea pink or thrift, is a flowering plant that belongs to the family Scrophulariaceae. The plant has been used for its medicinal properties, particularly for its antimicrobial and anti-inflammatory effects, which can be attributed to its high content of flavonoids and phenolic acids. The health benefits of Chaenorhinum litorale subsp. litorale include its potential to reduce inflammation, improve wound healing, and exhibit antimicrobial properties, making it a promising candidate for treating various health conditions. Herbal preparations of the plant, such as infusions, decoctions, and tinctures, can be used to harness its medicinal properties, although further research is needed to fully understand its potential as a therapeutic agent.
